name: Cascada de El Chorro — circular desde Mijares
region_intro: Hiking around Mijares is characterized by its diverse and mountainous terrain, significantly influenced by the Mijares River. The region features dramatic river gorges, lush valleys, and natural pools, alongside higher elevations within areas like the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park. Hikers can explore varied ecosystems, from riparian vegetation to cork oak forests and rugged mountain slopes. Best hiking trails around Mijares The most popular hiking route is Ruta del Tiétar – circular from Iglesuela del Tiétar (PR-TO 20), a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate path offers a circular journey through the Tiétar river valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cascada de El Chorro — circular desde Mijares, a moderate 9.8 miles (15.7 km) path. This route leads to the El Chorro waterfall, providing scenic views and a rewarding experience. Local hikers also love the Tordigas Waterfalls – Villanueva de Ávila loop from Villanueva de Ávila, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail leading through areas with waterfalls, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Hiking around Mijares is defined by mountainous terrain, river gorges, and cork oak forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Mijares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 350 hikers have used komoot to explore Mijares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Mijares?
A: Mijares offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 50 trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to different skill levels, including 15 easy, 30 moderate, and 5 difficult options.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Mijares?
A: The Mijares region is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. During warmer temperatures, the natural pools and thermal waters (around 25°C year-round in places like Montanejos) offer refreshing dips. The diverse ecosystems, from lush river valleys to cork oak forests, provide scenic beauty across seasons. For specific trail conditions, it's always good to check local weather forecasts.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to discover on hikes in Mijares?
A: Yes, Mijares is home to several beautiful waterfalls. You can explore routes that lead to them, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144782793/cascada-de-el-chorro-circular-desde-mijares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de El Chorro — circular desde Mijares&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable waterfalls include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4112342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the waterfalls near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6370654" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gavilanes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find natural swimming spots along the hiking trails in Mijares?
A: Absolutely. The Mijares River valley is known for its inviting natural swimming spots. Areas like La Rasclosa, Los Pertegaces, and Pozo de las Palomas are perfect for refreshing dips. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6327636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mijares natural pool&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21951047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gavilanes – Natural swimming pool loop from Gavilanes&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route specifically designed to visit one.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Mijares?
A: The hiking routes in Mijares are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from dramatic river gorges to lush valleys and cork oak forests, and the variety of trails suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Mijares?
A: Yes, Mijares offers several easy paths suitable for families. The region's river valleys provide gentle trails with picturesque riverside scenery. These routes allow families to enjoy nature without requiring extensive hiking experience.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Mijares?
A: While specific regulations can vary by trail or protected area, generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in Mijares, especially if kept on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage or park regulations, particularly within areas like the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, to ensure compliance.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Mijares?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Mijares are circular, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018292487/ruta-del-tietar-circular-from-iglesuela-del-tietar-pr-to-20" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Tiétar – circular from Iglesuela del Tiétar (PR-TO 20)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144782793/cascada-de-el-chorro-circular-desde-mijares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de El Chorro — circular desde Mijares&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What other natural landmarks can I explore while hiking in Mijares?
A: Beyond waterfalls and natural pools, Mijares boasts impressive natural landmarks. You can explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5765401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valley of the Garganta del Niño Cojo&lt;/a&gt;, reach summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5131972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de Gavilanes&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Torozo&lt;/a&gt;, or traverse mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2544554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Mijares&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2544555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serranillos Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What makes the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park a special hiking destination in Mijares?
A: The Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, located within the Alto Mijares region, is a significant cork oak forest known for its rich biodiversity. Hikers can enjoy spectacular trails through leafy forests, crossing rivers and ravines, and ascending to peaks over 1,000 meters. It's part of the Natura 2000 Network, protecting diverse wildlife including wild boars, roe deer, and various birds of prey.

Q: Are there opportunities for other outdoor activities in Mijares besides hiking?
A: Yes, Mijares is a hub for various outdoor pursuits. The Mijares River Narrows offers world-renowned climbing opportunities. Water sports like kayaking and paddle surfing are popular at the Sitjar Reservoir, and the Mijares River itself provides options for rafting. For the adventurous, wet caving in subterranean rivers and canyoning in various ravines are also available.

Q: Is there parking available at trailheads in Mijares?
A: While specific parking availability can vary by trailhead, many popular hiking areas and villages in Mijares offer designated parking spaces. It is generally advisable to arrive early, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot. Always check local information for specific routes if you have concerns about parking.
